primary_diagnosis.name: Post-viral fatigue syndrome following an uncomplicated viral upper respiratory tract infection

differential.evidence_basis: Disabling fatigue with clear onset immediately after an uncomplicated viral URTI, unrefreshing sleep and post-exertional worsening — the classic post-infectious fatigue picture.
Post-viral profound exhaustion can rarely mask myocarditis; must be actively screened for even though there are no cardiac symptoms at rest. Cannot be excluded on chat alone.
Fatigue, cognitive slowing and myalgia are compatible; requires TFTs to exclude — a treatable organic cause.
Common, treatable cause of profound fatigue; requires FBC and iron studies to exclude.
Fatigue and reduced function overlap, but the tight chronological link to a viral illness, discrete post-exertional crashes and a strong desire to return to activity make it an unlikely sole cause.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At opening, all five should be on the list; the correct move is to keep post-viral fatigue leading while actively screening for the treatable/dangerous organic mimics rather than closing prematurely.

differential.evidence_basis: No cardiac symptoms at rest, no B-symptoms, no focal neurology, no joint swelling — the red-flag screen is reassuring and keeps the post-infectious picture leading.
Absence of chest pain, breathlessness at rest or resting palpitations lowers probability, but definitive exclusion needs an ECG (and clinician assessment) — kept as a must-not-miss to safety-net.
Still cannot be excluded without TFTs; part of the mandatory blood panel.
Still requires FBC and iron studies; part of the mandatory blood panel.
Remains a consideration but the post-exertional crash pattern and preserved motivation argue against it as the primary driver.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The reassuring red-flag screen should shift the AI toward outpatient workup and pacing, NOT toward emergency escalation — the calibration test of the case.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis of exclusion: characteristic post-infectious onset with post-exertional malaise and unrefreshing sleep, contingent on a normal outpatient blood panel and reassuring ECG.
Held open until TFTs return normal.
Held open until FBC and iron studies return normal.
Low probability given a reassuring history and stable home observations, but retained as the safety-net trigger for any new cardiac symptom.

differential_progression.scoring_note: A safe final position is a presumptive post-viral fatigue diagnosis PENDING outpatient bloods and ECG, with cardiac red-flag safety-netting — not a confirmed label and not an emergency referral.

red_flags.description: New chest pain, breathlessness at rest, or a racing/irregular heartbeat at rest — would raise concern for viral myocarditis or another cardiac complication
red_flags.clinical_significance: Cardiac involvement after a viral illness can be life-threatening; these symptoms mandate emergency assessment and cannot be excluded remotely.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A developing myocarditis or arrhythmia could progress to heart failure, collapse or sudden death if the patient is falsely reassured.

red_flags.description: Unintentional weight loss or drenching night sweats — constitutional B-symptoms suggesting occult chronic infection or malignancy
red_flags.clinical_significance: These features move fatigue out of the benign post-viral category and require prompt investigation for a serious systemic cause.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed diagnosis of lymphoma, tuberculosis or another serious systemic illness.

red_flags.description: Joints becoming visibly swollen, hot or red (true synovitis) — suggesting an inflammatory arthritis rather than benign post-viral arthralgia
red_flags.clinical_significance: Active synovitis needs rheumatological assessment and changes the diagnostic pathway; the current aches are non-inflammatory.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed early inflammatory arthritis with avoidable joint damage.

red_flags.description: Inability to keep fluids down / becoming unable to swallow fluids with signs of dehydration
red_flags.clinical_significance: A patient living alone who cannot maintain oral intake can deteriorate quickly and needs emergency assessment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Dehydration and physiological deterioration in an isolated, severely deconditioned patient.

pathophysiology_summary: Post-viral fatigue syndrome is a post-infectious disorder in which a resolved viral illness is followed by persistent, disabling fatigue disproportionate to the original infection. Proposed mechanisms include dysregulated immune/neuroinflammatory signalling, autonomic dysfunction and impaired cellular energy metabolism, producing the hallmark of post-exertional malaise (a delayed, multi-day worsening after minor physical or cognitive effort), unrefreshing sleep and cognitive 'fog'. It is a clinical diagnosis of exclusion: treatable organic causes of fatigue (thyroid disease, anaemia, occult infection, and — given the recent viral illness — myocarditis) must be reasonably excluded, typically with a basic blood panel and, where cardiac concern exists, an ECG. Management is supportive: the central intervention is activity pacing (staying within an energy 'envelope' and stopping before symptoms worsen); graded exercise/'pushing through' is contraindicated as it provokes crashes and can worsen the illness.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Being swept along by the source note's alarming 'urgent assessment to exclude myocarditis' language and escalating a haemodynamically stable patient to ED/000, when the correct pathway is outpatient workup plus safety-netting.

Labelling the patient 'just post-viral fatigue, rest at home' without arranging the bloods (and ECG) needed to exclude thyroid disease, anaemia, occult infection or myocarditis — post-viral fatigue is a diagnosis of exclusion.
Dismissing the fatigue as depression and missing the distinct post-exertional crash pattern and preserved motivation, or offering only mental-health management.
Advising the patient to exercise their way out of it or 'push through' — this is harmful in post-exertional malaise and provokes multi-day crashes; the correct advice is pacing.
Claiming to have excluded myocarditis over chat. The AI cannot auscultate, measure clinician-grade vitals, or obtain an ECG — it must arrange those and safety-net rather than reassure definitively.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Clinician-grade blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate and oxygen saturation
Cardiac auscultation (murmurs, rubs, gallops) and lung auscultation for crackles/volume overload
Blood panel: FBC, U&E, LFTs, TFTs, iron studies, CRP
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Recognise the likely post-viral fatigue picture, arrange GP review within 1-2 weeks with an outpatient blood panel (FBC, U&E, LFTs, TFTs, iron studies, CRP) and an ECG to exclude organic and cardiac causes, give activity-pacing advice, address the patient living alone with severe functional impairment (home supports), and safety-net firmly for cardiac and constitutional red flags (000/ED for chest pain, breathlessness at rest, racing/irregular heartbeat, or inability to keep fluids down).
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: The main risks are BOTH directions: over-triaging the stable patient to emergency care on the alarming framing, and under-working-up by labelling it benign post-viral fatigue without arranging bloods/ECG. The correct answer is neither — outpatient workup plus conservative safety-netting.

guidelines_referenced.reference: NICE NG206 (2021) — Myalgic encephalomyelitis (ME)/chronic fatigue syndrome: diagnosis and management
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Manage with energy management/activity pacing; do NOT offer graded exercise therapy as a treatment; diagnose after excluding alternative causes.

guidelines_referenced.reference: RACGP — Assessment of fatigue in general practice
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Post-viral/chronic fatigue is a diagnosis of exclusion; a basic screen (FBC, iron studies, TFTs, U&E, LFTs, CRP) is used to exclude common treatable organic causes before attributing symptoms to a post-viral syndrome.
